name: ABBA Ecosystem Formation Engine
doctrine: Products do not invent separate doctrine. They are formed from the canonical Carbon Actual layers, swarm capabilities, app fractions, human roles, AI roles, delivery routes and proof rules.

formationStages.output: normalized product brief and measurable outcome
scope, exclusions, data classes, SEAL level and risk ceiling
ranked provider fractions with evidence and constraints
at least two comparable engine candidates where feasible
weighted performance, penalties, evidence and confidence
proof, pulse, KPI updates and authorized learning returned to swarms

selectionRules: Select fractions by verified capability and contextual rank, not provider popularity.
One provider may contribute several fractions and belong to several swarms.
Each team must include delivery, state, proof, security, fallback and human authority where required.
Use duplicate-function providers where useful to preserve competition, fallback and decentralization.
New and under-tested fractions receive controlled exploration opportunities.
Actual value is revealed only after ABBA resolution and SEAL approval.
Private product data does not return to swarms; only authorized learning, metadata and proof references return.

pilot.objective: onboard a learner, establish identity and consent, deliver orientation, assess readiness, issue a verifiable certification outcome, preserve continuity and expose only approved public proof
